Short description: Hold policy text constant while varying party cues to test whether AI coding tracks politically loaded labels rather than substantive content.
Empirical unit of analysis: Identical policy statements with manipulated party labels
Key typology question: Is construct work separable from perspective-aware bias auditing?
Observable artifacts / traces: Cue-perturbation experiments, confidence shifts, divergence from human labels
Key outcomes observed: Sensitivity to party cues, asymmetry across parties, stability of labels under perturbation
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Cue changes do not systematically move outputs
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Party labels can sometimes be substantively relevant context rather than pure bias
Why this case has high leverage: It makes model-mediated political bias visible at the measurement stage.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
